questions.explanation: A commit captures a snapshot of changes (with metadata and a message), enabling traceability and rollback—critical for controlled engineering changes.

options.A: To permanently delete files from the repository
options.B: To record a snapshot of changes with a message for traceability
options.C: To compile source code into a binary
options.D: To upload artifacts to an artifact repository

questions.question: In Git, what is the primary purpose of a commit?

questions.explanation: A Dockerfile contains the step-by-step instructions to build a Docker image (base image, dependencies, copy files, run commands, etc.).
questions.question: Which file is commonly used by Docker to define how to build a container image?

questions.explanation: SemVer increments MINOR for backward-compatible feature additions; MAJOR is for breaking changes and PATCH is for backward-compatible bug fixes.

options.D: None; versions should never change for new features

questions.question: In semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH), which part should change when you add a backward-compatible feature?

questions.explanation: Technical drawing tools produce standardized, dimensioned, and toleranced drawings that communicate design and manufacturing requirements clearly.

options.A: It replaces the need for measurements by automatically guessing dimensions
options.B: It creates standardized drawings (dimensions, tolerances, annotations) to communicate manufacturing intent
options.C: It is only used for marketing visuals, not production
options.D: It is primarily used to run CI/CD pipelines

questions.question: Which statement best describes the purpose of technical drawing software in an industrial engineering context?

questions.explanation: Maven is a Java build tool that resolves dependencies and produces artifacts like JARs in a repeatable way, commonly used in CI.
questions.question: You need a CI pipeline to build a Java service and produce a JAR with reproducible dependencies. Which build tool is most directly aligned with this requirement?

questions.explanation: df -h reports filesystem disk space usage in human-readable units, useful for diagnosing capacity issues affecting production systems.
questions.question: In Linux systems administration, which command is most appropriate to check disk space usage by mounted filesystems on a production server?

questions.explanation: Artifact repositories store and serve versioned build outputs with metadata and access control, enabling traceable, repeatable deployments.
questions.question: You want to publish and retrieve versioned build outputs (e.g., JARs, Docker images) for traceable releases across plants. Which tool category best fits this need?

questions.explanation: A Git tag plus embedding the commit SHA (e.g., OCI labels) creates a strong audit trail linking the built image to an immutable source revision.

options.A: Build from the default branch without tags and rely on timestamps
options.B: Tag the release in Git and embed the Git commit SHA as an image label during the build
options.C: Rename the Dockerfile after each release
options.D: Store the image only on the build agent to prevent changes

questions.question: A GitHub Actions workflow builds a Docker image for a line-control application. You must ensure the exact source state used for the image can be audited later. Which approach best supports this?

questions.explanation: Deployments with RollingUpdate and readiness probes gradually shift traffic only to healthy pods and support rollback to a previous ReplicaSet if issues occur.

options.B: Deployment with RollingUpdate strategy and readiness probes
options.D: Running pods only in the default namespace

questions.question: You manage a Kubernetes deployment for an industrial data-collection service. A new version causes intermittent failures under load. You need a safe rollout strategy that limits impact and allows quick rollback. Which Kubernetes feature best fits?

questions.explanation: Terraform is suited for declarative infrastructure provisioning, while Ansible handles configuration management; version control enables consistent, auditable environments.

options.A: Use Terraform to provision infrastructure and Ansible to configure OS/software, both driven by version-controlled code
options.B: Use only Bash scripts run manually on each server
options.C: Store environment details in a spreadsheet and apply changes ad hoc
options.D: Use npm to install system packages and ignore infrastructure provisioning

questions.question: You must provision identical test environments for a manufacturing execution system across multiple sites. Some resources are cloud (networks/VMs) and some are on-prem configuration steps (packages/services). Which approach best supports repeatability and separation of concerns?
